mappings.rationaleAI: Phonetics, syntax, semantics address the nature of language (C), definitions (C.1), ways of studying language (C.2), and linguistic change (C.7). Brain processing relates to phonetics (D.1). Linguistic theory development addresses F.1, F.2, F.3.
Language classification addresses definitions (C.1), dialects (C.3), and typology (C.7). Global distribution addresses classification (F.7) and languages of the world (H).

Language in sociocultural context addresses variants (C.3), language and culture (C.6), attitudes (G.1), history (G.2), cross-cultural relations (G.3), political instrument (G.4), and group unification (G.5).
English history addresses linguistic change (C.7) and Germanic languages (H.1.f).
Language diversity addresses language and culture (C.6), cross-cultural relations (G.3), political instrument (G.4), and languages of the world (H).
Nonstandard vocabulary addresses language variants (C.3) and group unification (G.5).
Translation transforming meaning addresses meaning and style (C.5) and cross-cultural relations (G.3).
Writing systems address origin (E.1), types (E.2), and systems (E.3).
Egyptian hieroglyphs address hieroglyphic systems (E.3) and calligraphy (E.5).
Letterform design addresses adjuncts to writing (E.4).
Literacy evolution addresses language and culture (C.6) and writing evolution (E.1).
Dictionaries address ways of studying language (C.2) and semantics (F.5).
Persuasion addresses communication as foundation of culture (A) and meaning and style (C.5).

mappings.vsiAuthor: Emma Borg and Sarah A. Fisher
mappings.rationaleAI: Word meaning and reference address meaning and style (C.5) and semantics (F.5).
Interpretation and meaning construction address meaning and style (C.5) and semantics (F.5).
Language processing addresses psychological bases of speech (C.4) and brain functions (D.2).
Reading disorder with brain differences addresses speech disorders (D.3).
Language acquisition addresses psychological bases of speech (C.4) and language transmission (C.6).
Ciphers and codes directly address cryptology (C.8).
AI language processing addresses cybernetic communication (B.4).
Programming languages address computer languages (B.4).
Information spanning communication addresses cultural foundation (A) and cybernetic communication (B.4).
Challenging fixed meanings addresses meaning and style (C.5) and semantics (F.5).
Structuralism and speech act theory address meaning (C.5) and semantics (F.5).
Chinese literary culture provides context for Chinese language (H.6.a).
Japanese literature provides context for the Japanese language (H.13).

Covers cultural systems, symbolic communication, and the construction of selfhood through ethnographic study including kinship and exchange systems, relevant to the use of signals, signs, symbols, icons, and cultural artifacts and attitudes toward language.

Covers the evolutionary origins and biological basis of emotions including the neural mechanisms of empathy and cross-cultural questions about emotional expression, relevant to communication by means of bodily gestures, posture, and facial expression.

Covers philosophical and psychological analysis of humor including stress relief, group cohesion, and cultural universality of humor across human societies, directly relevant to laughter and nonword sounds as communication.

Examines the relationship between thought and language, culturally specific modes of reasoning versus universal cognitive patterns, directly relevant to meaning and style in language and the relation of language and thought.

Covers cross-cultural literary relations and how comparative literature functions as an agent of international intellectual relations, directly relevant to the role of language in cross-cultural relations.
Covers Etruscan civilization including their language, which remains only partially deciphered and constitutes a language isolate, directly relevant to Etruscan as a language isolate.
